All right, welcome back to your next lesson on vision focus. Now, here's the deal. I was debating whether or not to actually include this next part in the training and the reason is, is because it's really more about me discussing what my vision is for the future. But as I thought about it, I realized it was actually some really incredibly valuable material. I'll tell you what it is. This footage was filmed a while back, you know, over five years ago, right? And you'll notice that what I said my vision was ended up going in a totally different direction in reality. In other words, I pivoted so many times from what I thought I would do. And what's the lesson there? Well, the lesson there is that your vision serves as a starting point. It's a living, breathing organism that tends to grow and evolve based on what's really happening. Remember that bifurcated focus? You want to focus on that vision, but I'm always scanning the horizon for opportunities along the way. So watch this video, all right, about five or six minutes and just focus on that. All right, let's cut to the live footage right now. Jordan, thank you so much. Just a final question. What's your personal vision for the future now? Where do you see yourself in the next few years? No, it's pretty interesting time for me because of, you know, so much going on right now and I have a movie coming out about my life with Leonardo DiCaprio's playing me. I'm still, I'm not even dead yet. I'm already got Leo playing me, right? So it's pretty interesting. You know, in a sense, I've been struggling. It's a good question over the last, you know, three to six months of really saying to myself, what's next for me? Because I've achieved pretty much everything I've wanted in my life right now at this point. You know, I was coming from such a bad spot when I lost everything. And then I made all my money back and, you know, started empowering lots of people around the world. And I started just feeling so good that it would be easy for me to fall into the trap of saying I just want more of the same. Truth is, I think that I'm better than that for myself. I think I owe myself more. I think I owe the world more. I think I certainly have some great gifts I was given from God or nature, whatever they are, the ability to inspire people, to empower people. And I think that it would be unfair of me to not sort of share that at a higher level. You know, in fact, one of the reasons why I'm doing this around the corporate world is because I couldn't possibly service all the people around the world. And I'm one person. I can't go training, you know, five million people, right? So this product itself allows me to leverage myself, to get the message out there, to be able to teach people how to, you know, become closers, how to become ethical closers and how to become visionaries in their own life and how to inspire themselves and inspire other people, right? But I think that's just, you know, one element of my life. I think the next great element for me is to really expand on my charity work that I do. I do a lot of stuff in Africa right now with impoverished kids. And I actually go in there and use these same lessons and these same strategies and skill sets to, you know, give hope sometimes to what's the hopeless and hopefully inspire these kids and make a change. And at least some of them have helped break the cycle of poverty. So, you know, for me, you know, I travel so much around the world. I'm constantly jet-lagged and I always find that matter how jet-lagged I am. When I find myself in a township working with kids, I feel amazing. It feeds my spirit at the highest level. So I think there's a lot more of that in my future. And I think I really am at that point right now where probably, you know, we'll have to sort of re-clarify my own vision for the future. So, you know, at this point, I would say I'm probably got another three to six months on autopilot where I'm at now because, you know, I'm living my vision. And then I think, you know, you know, I'm always searching around and saying, you know, what's the next conquest? You know, what's the next thing? I don't think I'll, you know, do a Richard Branson and try to blast off into space and try to, you know, put a colony on the moon. I love Richard. He's a friend of mine. I think my future probably lies on the planet still, you know, but maybe in some of the continents like Africa and, you know, the places around the world where the kids are really, really sorely lacking in the sort of skill sets I know that I can impart on them that will empower them and really help these communities break the cycles of poverty and really make a change. And that to me would be, you know, huge success and a great sort of finale to my life because I've kind of pretty much lived 10 or 12 lives depending on who you speak to. So basically there are three things that you have to know how to do. If you want to make money, if you want to help people, if you want to break out of your, you know, the confines of where you are and go to the next level. I'll tell you what they are. Number one, it all starts with this. Starts with what's called having a vision for your future. A vision for your future. That's the first thing that you need to have. If you don't have a vision, you cannot succeed at any reasonable level. You can make a little bit of money and you can have a bit of success in your life. But you can never really get anywhere amazing. It all starts with a vision. Essentially in your mind to know where you want to go in life before you actually get there. So first it has to inspire you and secondly it has to inspire the people around you. You see if it's just inspired you, it's all about you making some money, that doesn't bring other people into it. It's just inspired you first and then the people around you. The second step is you have to learn how to share that vision with other people. How to share that vision with other people. What do I mean by that? Let me explain. In other words, you might have this amazing, compelling, bold, bright vision for your future. It could be starting a small business. It could be starting a bigger business. It could be working in your community, helping people, assisting people who need to be educated, to be inspired. You could have this amazing vision of what you want to do. But if you don't have the ability to essentially get other people to listen to you, to share that vision, then you're a one trick show. It's just you against the whole world. Great leaders have the ability to speak, to share, through words, their vision and inspire people. An example would be who? Nelson Mandela. It's the ability, not just, you know, he could have had the greatest vision for South Africa, to unite the country, to make it an amazing place. But if he couldn't share that vision with other people in a way that inspired it, that made him want to listen to it, that made him want to buy into it, to become part of it, with their time, their money, their hard work. That Nelson Mandela's vision could have never become a reality. So A, you need the vision. And then B, you need to learn.
